product_overview: MCPForge is a web app that converts OpenAPI specifications into production-ready MCP server projects via a visual interface. Users can register, create projects by uploading/pasting/fetching specs, map endpoints to MCP primitives, configure server settings, preview generated code, and download complete project zips.
core_goals: Enable developers to convert OpenAPI specs to MCP servers without CLI tooling
Provide a visual interface for mapping API endpoints to MCP primitives (Tool, Resource, Resource Template, Exclude)
Generate production-ready, downloadable MCP server projects with configurable transport and auth options
Allow users to manage multiple projects, maintain version snapshots, and preview generated code

features.name: User Sign Up
features.description: Register a new account with name (optional), email, password and confirm password, with client-side validation and auto sign-in on success.
user_flows: Navigate to /auth/signup -> Optionally enter full name -> Enter email address -> Enter password meeting requirements (min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 number) -> Enter confirm password matching password -> Click "Create Account" button -> See inline validation disappear -> See automatic sign-in and redirect to /dashboard -> See dashboard content (empty state or projects grid)

Navigate to /auth/signup -> Enter invalid email format -> Enter weak password (no uppercase) -> Click "Create Account" button -> See inline validation errors under email and password fields -> Fix password but use an email already registered -> Click "Create Account" button -> See server error toast "Email already registered" and remain on /auth/signup

features.name: User Sign In
features.description: Authenticate with email and password using NextAuth credentials provider and receive JWT session for authenticated routes.
user_flows: Navigate to /auth/signin -> Enter email address -> Enter password -> Click "Sign In" button -> See loading state on button -> On success, redirected to /dashboard -> See project cards
Navigate to /auth/signin -> Enter email address -> Enter wrong password -> Click "Sign In" button -> See error message "Invalid email or password" and remain on /auth/signin

features.name: User Sign Out (Logout)
features.description: Sign out from the navbar user dropdown, clearing session and redirecting to sign-in page.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> Click avatar/user dropdown in the top-right navbar -> Click "Sign out" button -> See redirect to /auth/signin -> Attempt to access /dashboard and get redirected to /auth/signin
Navigate to /dashboard -> Click avatar/user dropdown -> Click "Sign out" button -> Network error occurs during signOut -> See error toast and remain signed in

features.name: Landing Page
features.description: Public marketing landing page with hero, workflow, code preview, features grid and CTAs linking to sign up or sign in.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See hero section, three-step workflow and code output preview -> Click "Start Building - Free" -> Navigate to /auth/signup
Navigate to / -> Click "Sign in" in the nav bar -> Navigate to /auth/signin -> Attempt to access /auth/signin while already authenticated -> Get redirected to /dashboard

features.name: Navbar with User Menu
features.description: Persistent navigation bar with logo link, Dashboard link and user avatar dropdown showing name, email and sign-out action.
user_flows: On any authenticated page (e.g., /dashboard) -> Click MCPForge logo in navbar -> Navigate to /dashboard
On /dashboard while logged in -> Click avatar/name to open dropdown -> See name and email displayed -> Click "Sign out" to log out

features.name: Dashboard (Project List)
features.description: List all user projects as responsive cards showing project name, spec title, endpoint count and last updated time with create and delete actions.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> See loading skeleton then project cards in a responsive grid -> Click a project card -> Navigate to /projects/[id]

Navigate to /dashboard when user has no projects -> See empty state message "No projects yet. Create your first MCPForge project to get started." -> Click "Create Project" button -> Navigate to /projects/new

features.name: Search Projects
features.description: Debounced client-side search on the dashboard that filters projects by name or spec title in real time.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> Type a search query in the "Search projects..." input -> Wait 300ms debounce -> See project cards filtered by name/spec title in real time
Navigate to /dashboard -> Type a search query with no matches -> Wait 300ms debounce -> See "No results" empty state -> Click clear (X) -> See full project list restored

features.name: Delete Project
features.description: Delete a project from the dashboard with confirmation dialog and cascading removal of mappings, config and versions.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> Hover over a project card to reveal the trash icon button -> Click the trash icon -> See confirmation dialog titled "Delete Project" with warning message -> Click "Delete" to confirm -> See success toast "Project deleted" -> Project card removed from grid
Navigate to /dashboard -> Hover over a project card and click trash icon -> Click "Delete" -> Server returns error -> See error toast "Failed to delete project" and project card remains

features.name: Create Project - Upload File Tab
features.description: Create a new project by uploading a .json, .yaml, or .yml OpenAPI spec via drag-and-drop or file picker (max 5MB).
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter a project name in the "Project Name" field -> Ensure "Upload File" tab is selected -> Drag and drop a valid openapi.json file under 5MB into the drop zone -> See selected file name and size displayed -> Click "Create Project" button -> See "Creating..." button state -> On success see toast and redirect to /projects/[id]

Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter a project name -> Select "Upload File" tab -> Upload a .txt file -> See validation error "Only .json, .yaml, and .yml files are accepted" -> Replace file with valid file or cancel

features.name: Create Project - Paste Content Tab
features.description: Create a new project by pasting raw OpenAPI JSON or YAML content into a textarea with character count and validation.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter a project name -> Click the "Paste Content" tab -> Paste valid OpenAPI JSON into the textarea -> See character count update -> Click "Create Project" button -> On success see toast and redirect to /projects/[id]

Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter a project name -> Click the "Paste Content" tab -> Paste invalid JSON into textarea -> Click "Create Project" -> See parsing error alert "Invalid JSON syntax: {error message}" and remain on page

features.name: Create Project - Fetch from URL Tab
features.description: Create a new project by fetching an OpenAPI spec from a remote URL with a 10-second timeout and preview.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter a project name -> Click "Fetch from URL" tab -> Enter a valid OpenAPI spec URL -> Click "Fetch" -> See loading spinner on button -> See "Spec fetched successfully" message and preview of first 2000 characters -> Click "Create Project" -> On success see toast and redirect to /projects/[id]

Navigate to /projects/new -> Enter a project name -> Click "Fetch from URL" tab -> Enter an invalid URL string -> Click "Fetch" -> See validation error "Invalid URL format" -> Enter a valid URL but request times out -> See error message "Request timed out after 10 seconds"

features.name: Endpoint Explorer Table
features.description: Interactive table listing parsed endpoints with method badges, path, operationId, summary, params count, and MCP type dropdown that supports search, filtering, selection and pagination.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Ensure "Endpoints" tab is selected -> See summary bar with totals -> See table rows with method badges, path, operationId and MCP type dropdowns -> Scroll horizontally on small screens if needed
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Ensure "Endpoints" tab is selected -> Apply filters that yield no results -> See empty state "No endpoints match filters" displayed

features.name: Endpoint Search and Filters
features.description: Filter endpoint table by search (path or operationId), HTTP method, and MCP type with 300ms debounce and combined AND logic.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> In the Endpoints tab type in the "Search by path or operation..." input -> Wait 300ms debounce -> See table filtered by matching path or operationId
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Select an HTTP method from the "All Methods" dropdown and an MCP type from the "All Types" dropdown -> See table apply both filters with AND logic -> See page reset to 1

features.name: Endpoint MCP Type Change (Single)
features.description: Change the MCP type of a single endpoint via inline dropdown with optimistic UI update and revert on failure.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> In the Endpoints table click the MCP type dropdown on a row -> Select "Tool" -> See optimistic update immediately in the row -> See spinner icon while saving -> On success see summary counts refresh and green checkmark animation

Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click MCP type dropdown on a row -> Select "Resource" -> Optimistic update shows new type -> Server returns error -> See error toast "Failed to save" -> Dropdown reverts to previous value

features.name: Endpoint Bulk Actions
features.description: Select multiple endpoints and change their MCP type in bulk, including select-all on current page and result toasts.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> In the Endpoints table click individual row checkboxes to select 3 endpoints -> See bulk action bar appear with count of selected items -> Click "Set as Tool" -> See loading spinner during bulk update -> On success see toast "Updated 3 endpoints" -> Selection clears and table refreshes

Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click header checkbox to select all visible rows -> Click "Set as Resource" -> Server returns partial failure -> See error toast and table refresh shows unchanged rows where update failed

features.name: Endpoint Pagination
features.description: Navigate through pages of endpoints when total exceeds 20 per page with page info and previous/next controls.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> In Endpoints tab see "Showing 1-20 of Z endpoints" and "Page 1 of M" -> Click next page button -> See new API call with page parameter and updated rows for page 2
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Attempt to click previous page when on first page -> See previous button disabled and no page change

features.name: Server Config Panel
features.description: Side panel for configuring generated MCP server transport, auth, server metadata and port with auto-save (500ms debounce) and validation.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> In Endpoints tab open right-side Server Config panel -> Change transport to "SSE (Hono)" -> See port field appear -> Enter port 4000 -> See "Saving..." then "Saved" status after debounce
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> In Server Config panel enter invalid port 999 -> See immediate validation error "Port must be between 1024 and 65535" and auto-save prevented until fixed

features.name: Code Preview
features.description: Preview generated MCP server code across file tabs with syntax highlighting, line numbers, file size, copy-to-clipboard and live regeneration on changes.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click the "Preview" tab -> See loading skeleton while code is generated -> See index.ts tab selected with syntax-highlighted code and file size -> Click copy icon -> See checkmark for 2 seconds and toast "Copied to clipboard"
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Make mapping or config change -> See brief "Regenerating..." overlay -> Code generation fails -> See error message in preview pane

features.name: Download Project
features.description: Generate and download the complete MCP server project as a zip, creating a version snapshot and triggering browser download.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click "Download Project" button in header -> Button changes to "Generating..." with spinner -> Browser automatically downloads mcpforge-{project-name-kebab}.zip -> See toast "Project downloaded"
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click "Download Project" -> Generation API returns error -> See error toast "Failed to generate project" and no download

features.name: Version History
features.description: List of generated version snapshots with summary and expandable detailed snapshot view (read-only).
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click the "History" tab -> See list of versions ordered by most recent with version number, summary and timestamp -> Click a version row to expand detailed config snapshot
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click the "History" tab for a project with no versions -> See empty state "No versions yet. Download your project to create the first version."

features.name: Inline Project Name Editing
features.description: Edit the project name inline on the project detail header with Enter to save and Escape to cancel.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click the pencil icon next to project name -> See inline input auto-focused and pre-selected -> Edit the project name -> Press Enter -> See loading spinner while saving -> On success see toast and updated header name
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Click pencil icon -> Modify name -> Press Escape -> See input cancel and original name restored

routes.description: Landing page with hero section, three-step flow, code output preview, features grid, and CTA
Sign in page with email and password credentials form
Sign up page with name, email, password, and confirm password form with client-side validation
Dashboard listing all user projects as cards with search, delete, and create new project actions
Create new project page with three input tabs for OpenAPI spec (upload file, paste content, fetch from URL)
Project detail page with endpoint explorer table, server config panel, code preview, and version history tabs
NextAuth API route handler for session management and credential-based authentication
POST endpoint for user registration with email/password validation and bcrypt hashing
GET lists all user projects; POST creates a new project by parsing an OpenAPI spec and creating mappings and config
GET returns single project with mappings and config; PATCH updates project name; DELETE removes project
GET returns paginated endpoint mappings with search, method, and mcpType filtering
PATCH updates a single endpoint mapping mcpType (tool, resource, resource_template, exclude)
PATCH bulk updates mcpType for multiple selected endpoint mappings
GET returns server config; PUT updates server config (transport, auth, name, version, baseUrl, port)
POST generates MCP server code files from mappings and config, creates a version snapshot
GET generates and returns a zip file of the complete MCP server project
GET lists all version snapshots for a project ordered by most recent

features.name: User Sign Up
features.description: Register a new account with name (optional), email, password, and confirm password. Client-side validation enforces minimum 8 characters, 1 uppercase, 1 number. Auto-signs in after successful registration.
user_interactions: Navigate to /auth/signup or click "Get started" / "Sign up" link
Optionally enter full name
Enter email address
Enter password (min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 number)
Enter confirm password
Click "Create Account" button
See validation errors inline if any field is invalid
On success, automatically signed in and redirected to /dashboard

features.name: User Sign In
features.description: Authenticate with email and password using NextAuth credentials provider. JWT session strategy.
user_interactions: Navigate to /auth/signin or click "Sign in" link
Enter email address
Enter password
Click "Sign In" button
See error message if credentials are invalid
On success, redirected to /dashboard

features.name: User Sign Out (Logout)
features.description: Sign out via the navbar dropdown menu. Uses NextAuth signOut server action and redirects to sign in page.
user_interactions: Click avatar/user dropdown in the top-right navbar
Click "Sign out" button in dropdown menu
Redirected to /auth/signin

features.name: Dashboard (Project List)
features.description: View all projects as a grid of cards showing project name, spec title, endpoint count, and last updated time. Includes loading skeleton states and empty state.
user_interactions: View project cards in a responsive grid (1-3 columns)
See project name, spec title, endpoint count, and relative update time on each card
Click a project card to navigate to /projects/[id]
Click "New Project" button to navigate to /projects/new

features.name: Search Projects
features.description: Filter projects on the dashboard by name or spec title with debounced client-side search (300ms debounce).
user_interactions: Type a search query in the "Search projects..." input field
See project cards filtered in real time (client-side)
See "No results" empty state if no projects match

features.name: Delete Project
features.description: Delete a project from the dashboard with a confirmation dialog. Removes project and all associated mappings, config, and versions via cascade.
user_interactions: Hover over a project card to reveal the trash icon button
Click the trash icon to open confirmation dialog
Read the warning that deletion is permanent and includes all mappings and configs
Click "Cancel" to dismiss or "Delete" to confirm
See toast notification on success or failure
Project card removed from grid on success

features.name: Create Project - Upload File Tab
features.description: Create a new project by uploading a .json, .yaml, or .yml OpenAPI spec file via drag-and-drop or file picker. Max 5MB.
user_interactions: Enter a project name in the "Project Name" field
Select the "Upload File" tab (default)
Drag and drop a file onto the drop zone, or click to open file picker
See selected file name and size displayed
Click the X button to remove file and select a different one
See validation errors for wrong file type or size exceeding 5MB
Click "Create Project" button
On success, see toast and redirect to /projects/[id]

features.name: Create Project - Paste Content Tab
features.description: Create a new project by pasting raw OpenAPI JSON or YAML content into a textarea.
user_interactions: Enter a project name in the "Project Name" field
Click the "Paste Content" tab
Paste OpenAPI spec content (JSON or YAML) into the textarea
See character count displayed below textarea
Click "Create Project" button
On success, see toast and redirect to /projects/[id]

features.name: Create Project - Fetch from URL Tab
features.description: Create a new project by fetching an OpenAPI spec from a remote URL with 10-second timeout.
user_interactions: Enter a project name in the "Project Name" field
Click the "Fetch from URL" tab
Enter the URL of an OpenAPI spec
Click the "Fetch" button
See loading spinner while fetching
See "Spec fetched successfully" message with preview of first 2000 characters
See error if URL is invalid, request fails, or times out after 10 seconds
Click "Create Project" button
On success, see toast and redirect to /projects/[id]

features.name: Endpoint Explorer Table
features.description: Interactive table showing all parsed API endpoints with method badges, path, operation ID, summary, param count, and MCP type dropdown. Supports search, filtering, selection, and pagination.
user_interactions: View summary bar showing total endpoints, tools, resources, templates, and excluded counts
See table with columns for checkbox, method badge (color-coded), path, operation ID, summary, params count, and MCP type dropdown
Scroll horizontally on smaller screens
View loading skeleton while data loads
See empty state when no endpoints match filters

features.name: Endpoint Search and Filters
features.description: Filter endpoint table by search query (path or operation ID), HTTP method dropdown, and MCP type dropdown. Search is debounced at 300ms.
user_interactions: Type in the "Search by path or operation..." input
Select an HTTP method from the "All Methods" dropdown (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE)
Select an MCP type from the "All Types" dropdown (Tool, Resource, Resource Template, Exclude)
Filters apply in real time; page resets to 1 on filter change
Clear filters by selecting "All Methods" or "All Types"

features.name: Endpoint MCP Type Change (Single)
features.description: Change the MCP type of a single endpoint mapping via an inline dropdown with optimistic UI update.
user_interactions: Click the MCP type dropdown on any endpoint row
Select a new type (Tool, Resource, Resource Template, or Exclude)
See optimistic update immediately in the table
See spinner icon while saving
Summary counts refresh after save
See error toast and revert on failure

features.name: Endpoint Bulk Actions
features.description: Select multiple endpoints via checkboxes and change their MCP type in bulk. Supports select-all on current page.
user_interactions: Click individual row checkboxes to select endpoints
Click the header checkbox to select/deselect all on current page (supports indeterminate state)
See bulk action bar appear showing count of selected items
Click "Set as Tool", "Set as Resource", "Set as Template", or "Exclude" button
See loading spinner during bulk update
See success toast with count of updated endpoints
Selection clears and table refreshes after bulk update

features.name: Endpoint Pagination
features.description: Navigate through pages of endpoints when total exceeds 20 per page. Shows page info and previous/next controls.
user_interactions: See "Showing X-Y of Z endpoints" text and "Page N of M" indicator
Click previous page button (disabled on first page)
Click next page button (disabled on last page)
Page changes trigger new API call with updated page parameter

features.name: Server Config Panel
features.description: Side panel on the project detail Endpoints tab for configuring generated MCP server settings. Auto-saves with 500ms debounce.
user_interactions: View config panel on the right side of the Endpoints tab
Select transport type via radio buttons (stdio, SSE, Streamable HTTP)
See port number field appear when SSE or Streamable HTTP is selected
Enter port number (1024-65535)
Select auth method from dropdown (None, API Key, Bearer Token, Basic Auth)
See note about .env credentials when auth is not "none"
Edit server name (max 50 chars)
Edit server version (semver x.y.z)
Edit base URL
See "Saving...", "Saved", or "Failed to save" status indicator
All changes auto-save with 500ms debounce
See field-level validation errors from server

features.name: Code Preview
features.description: View generated MCP server code across five file tabs (index.ts, package.json, tsconfig.json, .env.example, README.md) with syntax highlighting, line numbers, file sizes, and copy-to-clipboard.
user_interactions: Click the "Preview" tab on the project detail page
See generated code auto-load with loading skeleton
Switch between file tabs (index.ts, package.json, tsconfig.json, .env.example, README.md)
See file size in each tab header
View syntax-highlighted code with line numbers
Click the copy icon button to copy file content to clipboard
See checkmark icon after successful copy
See error message if generation fails

features.name: Download Project
features.description: Generate and download the complete MCP server project as a zip file. Triggers code generation, creates a version snapshot, then downloads the zip.
user_interactions: Click the "Download" button in the project detail header
See button change to "Generating..." with spinner
Browser automatically downloads mcpforge-[project-name].zip file
See toast notification "Project downloaded" on success
See error toast if generation or download fails

features.name: Version History
features.description: View all generated version snapshots of a project. Each version shows version number, config summary (tools, resources, transport), and timestamp. Expandable to show detailed configuration snapshot.
user_interactions: Click the "History" tab on the project detail page
See list of versions ordered by most recent, each showing version number, summary, and date
See empty state message if no versions exist yet
Click a version row to expand/collapse detailed snapshot view
View transport, tool count, resource count, and file count in expanded view

features.name: Inline Project Name Editing
features.description: Edit the project name directly on the project detail page header via inline input with Enter to save and Escape to cancel.
user_interactions: Click the pencil icon next to the project name
See inline input field replace the name text, auto-focused and pre-selected
Edit the project name
Press Enter or click the check icon to save
Press Escape or click the X icon to cancel
See loading spinner on check icon while saving
See toast notification on success or failure
Name reverts if save fails

features.name: Landing Page
features.description: Public marketing landing page showcasing MCPForge with hero section, three-step workflow explanation, production-ready output preview, features grid (8 feature cards), and bottom CTA. Includes navigation links to sign in and sign up.
user_interactions: View hero section with tagline and call-to-action buttons
Click "Start Building - Free" to navigate to /auth/signup
Click "View Documentation" link
Click "Sign in" in the nav bar to go to /auth/signin
Click "Get started" in the nav bar to go to /auth/signup
Click GitHub icon to open GitHub repository
Scroll through three-step flow, code output section, and features grid
Click "Get started free" in bottom CTA to go to /auth/signup

features.name: Navbar with User Menu
features.description: Persistent navigation bar shown on authenticated pages with logo link, Dashboard link, and user avatar dropdown menu showing name, email, and sign-out action.
user_interactions: Click MCPForge logo to go to /dashboard (or / if not signed in)
Click "Dashboard" link to go to /dashboard
Click avatar/name to open dropdown menu
View name and email in dropdown
Click "Sign out" to log out

known_limitations.issue: Forgot password link is non-functional (points to
GitHub OAuth sign-in is commented out

known_limitations.impact: Not suitable for multi-instance deployment or high concurrency

known_limitations.issue: URL fetch for OpenAPI spec happens client-side without CORS proxy
known_limitations.impact: Many API spec URLs will fail due to CORS restrictions in the browser
Any email address can be used without verification

known_limitations.issue: Version history shows snapshots but does not support restoring to a previous version
known_limitations.impact: Version history is read-only; users cannot rollback to earlier configurations
